title: SACRAMENTO – As highly destructive vegetation fires start off the summer in California, Senator Dave Cortese’s (D-Silicon Valley) bill developed in response to the fires was passed
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: Senator Dave Cortese's bill SB 653 is designed to define vegetation management practices that reduce wildfire risk.
SB 653 supports native wildlife and plant health through its defined vegetation management practices.
The bill encourages the use of environmentally sensitive vegetation management (ESVM) practices.
ESVM practices maintain ecological health and biodiversity while reducing long-term catastrophic wildfire risk.
Senator Cortese's legislation SB 429 creates the nation’s first public wildfire catastrophe model to improve insurance transparency.
SB 616 establishes a Community Hardening Commission to unify and modernize home fire protection standards.
